data.title: Bitcoin Futures Open Interest Hits 14-Month Low Amid Institutional Strategy Shift
data.description:
On April 11, the open interest in Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME) Bitcoin futures fell to $8.41 billion, marking its lowest level in 14 months. According to BlockBeats, Glassnode analysts attribute this decline primarily to the unwinding of basis trades. Previously, this strategy involved establishing long positions through spot ETFs and hedging with short futures to earn the spread. However, the annualized returns have recently dropped from 15%-20% to around 5%, prompting institutions to take profits.

Additionally, the daily trading volume of CME Bitcoin futures has decreased to below $3 billion. Analysts suggest that as institutional demand shifts towards directly holding spot assets, the leverage in the futures market is significantly decreasing.

data.title: Iran's Key Concerns Highlighted Amid Regional Tensions
data.description: Iran's national television has reported on the country's critical issues amid ongoing regional tensions. According to Odaily, Iran's red lines include the strategic Strait of Hormuz, the payment of war reparations, the unfreezing of Iranian assets, and a ceasefire across the region.

data.title: AI TRENDS | UK Central Bank to Discuss Impact of Anthropic's AI Model
data.description: On April 11, Jin10 reported that the Bank of England plans to discuss the impact of Anthropic's newly launched AI model with financial institutions. According to Jin10, UK regulators are joining their counterparts in the US and other countries in warning about the risks posed by this tool. Anthropic's Mythos model will be included in the agenda for the Bank of England's upcoming 'Cross-Market Operational Resilience Group' and 'CMORG AI Special Task Force' meetings, scheduled to take place within the next two weeks. The Federal Reserve and the Treasury Department have already held emergency meetings on this issue, and the Bank of Canada convened a meeting with banks and financial companies last Friday to discuss the cybersecurity risks associated with Mythos. These meetings reflect growing concerns among regulators that a new type of cyberattack is becoming one of the biggest risks facing the financial industry.

data.title: Iranian media: Iran's red lines include the Strait of Hormuz, war reparations, the unfreezing of frozen assets, and a ceasefire across the region.
data.description: According to a reporter from Iranian state television, Iran's red lines include the Strait of Hormuz, war reparations payments, the unfreezing of frozen Iranian assets, and a ceasefire throughout the region. (Jinshi)

data.title: AI TRENDS | U.S. Officials Meet Wall Street Leaders Over AI Cyber Risks
data.description: U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent held an urgent meeting with Wall Street leaders this week to address AI-driven cyber risks associated with Anthropic's Mythos model. According to NS3.AI, Anthropic reported that Mythos identified thousands of high-severity vulnerabilities and has restricted access through its Project Glasswing initiative. This meeting follows actions by U.S. agencies to limit the government's use of Anthropic products.

data.title: Iranian government spokesperson: Iran will participate in the negotiations with the utmost caution.
data.description: On November 11 local time, Iranian government spokesperson Mohajrani stated that Iran's dispatch of a diplomatic team led by Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf to participate in the US-Iran negotiations demonstrates Iran's willingness to engage in dialogue. She emphasized that, as the Iranian president previously stated, Iran will negotiate with its "finger on the trigger." While Iran is willing to engage in dialogue, it lacks trust in the other side; therefore, the Iranian diplomatic team will participate in the negotiations with the utmost caution. Mohajrani expressed hope that, under the guidance of the Supreme Leader, the Iranian negotiating team will achieve success. (CCTV News)

data.title: Iran-US Negotiations Progress Slower Than Anticipated
data.description: According to NS3.AI, a Pakistani government official has indicated that the ongoing negotiations between Iran and the United States are progressing at a slower pace than initially expected. The official noted that while the U.S. side seems eager to achieve specific objectives, Iran is taking a more measured approach and is not rushing to reach a conclusion.

data.title: Vance holds talks with Pakistani Prime Minister
data.description: According to the White House, U.S. Vice President Vance held talks with the Prime Minister of Pakistan on the 11th local time. (CCTV News)

data.title: White House: US officials deny US has agreed to unfreeze Iranian assets
data.description: The White House: US officials deny that the US has agreed to unfreeze Iranian assets. (Jinshi)

data.title: Iranian sources say the conditions for starting negotiations have not yet been met.
data.description: On the 11th local time, Iranian sources stated that the conditions set by Iran for initiating negotiations, including the unfreezing of Iranian assets and a ceasefire in Lebanon, have not yet been met. However, earlier, a senior Iranian source indicated that the United States had agreed to unfreeze Iranian assets "in Qatar and other countries' banks." The United States has not yet made any public response regarding the unfreezing of Iranian assets. Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf posted on social media on the 10th that two conditions must be met before negotiations can begin: a ceasefire in Lebanon and the unfreezing of Iranian assets. (CCTV News)

data.title: Analysis: Institutional investors show insufficient interest in allocating Bitcoin; the situation in Iran becomes a key variable.
data.description: While Bitcoin has risen nearly 7% since Sunday, its momentum slowed after reaching the $72,000 range, with institutional funds generally lacking a clear direction. The market faces key uncertainty: the progress of US-Iran negotiations over the weekend. The options market shows that institutions are betting on Bitcoin's upward movement through call options; for example, call options with an $80,000 strike price on Deribit are popular. Demand for call options linked to the BlackRock Bitcoin ETF (IBIT) remains strong. Furthermore, demand for downside protection remains robust, with no significant decrease in put option allocations. On the macro level, rising energy prices may strengthen expectations of a Federal Reserve rate hike, thus putting pressure on risk assets, including Bitcoin. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Pakistani government official: Negotiations between Iran and the United States are progressing slower than expected.
data.description: A Pakistani government official stated on the 11th that negotiations between Iran and the United States are progressing more slowly than expected and may continue for a longer period. Currently, the US appears eager to achieve a certain objective, while Iran seems less impatient. (Xinhua International)

data.title: Bitdeer Sells All Mined Bitcoin, Maintains Zero-BTC Holding Strategy
data.description: Bitdeer, a Nasdaq-listed cryptocurrency mining company, has sold all 165 Bitcoin it mined this week. According to NS3.AI, the company is adhering to its strategy of maintaining zero Bitcoin holdings. This approach reflects Bitdeer's ongoing commitment to liquidating its mined assets immediately, rather than holding onto them for potential future gains. The decision aligns with the company's financial strategy and market outlook.

data.title: Crypto Trader 'Maji' Increases Long Positions Amid Market Fluctuations
data.description:
On April 11, 'Maji' continued to strategically increase long positions, according to BlockBeats. The total value of these positions has risen to $42.17 million, up from approximately $34 million the previous evening. These positions are reported to have a high safety margin, with liquidation prices significantly lower than current market values.

As of the latest update, 'Maji' holds detailed positions including a 25x leverage on Ethereum with 12,131.7179 ETH at a liquidation price of $2,090.95, a 40x leverage on Bitcoin with 180 BTC at a liquidation price of $62,529.3, and a 10x leverage on HYPE with 43,750 units.

Additionally, 'Maji' has achieved a total contract profit of $1.85 million over the past week, and has turned a monthly loss into a profit of $190,000.

data.title: A whale withdrew 265,100 LINK tokens from Binance, worth $2.38 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, three hours ago, a whale withdrew 265,100 LINK tokens from Binance, worth $2.38 million.

data.title: Joe Lubin Advocates for Ethereum-Based Strategy Model
data.description: Joe Lubin has proposed that an Ethereum version of the Strategy model could be more effective than merely holding Bitcoin. According to NS3.AI, Lubin suggests that this approach would involve continuously accumulating and staking ETH, with all funds allocated to ETH from the outset to maximize compounded returns.

data.title: U.S. Agrees to Unfreeze Iranian Assets in Foreign Banks
data.description: The United States has agreed to lift the freeze on Iranian assets held in banks in Qatar and other foreign countries. According to BlockBeats, this development was confirmed by a senior Iranian official. The decision marks a significant step in the ongoing negotiations between the two nations. Further details on the implementation of this agreement have not been disclosed.

data.title: Ethereum co-founder Joe Lubin: The "Ethereum version of the Strategy model" can be done better.
data.description: Bitmine Chairman Tom Lee shared an interview video of Ethereum co-founder Joe Lubin on the X platform. Lubin stated that after meeting with Michael Saylor, founder of the Bitcoin treasury company Strategy, he discovered that the "Ethereum version of the Strategy model" could be done even better. Using Bitmine and Tom Lee's model as examples, Lubin explained that by continuously accumulating and staking ETH, the core strategy is to allocate 100% of funds to ETH and participate in staking to generate returns, achieving compound growth from day one. Compared to simply holding Bitcoin, Ethereum can achieve asset appreciation and reallocation through staking returns and a multi-layered reward mechanism within its ecosystem, driving the balance sheet to shift from a "holding-oriented" to a "return + allocation-oriented" model.

data.title: TD Cowen Analyst Lowers Target Price for MicroStrategy
data.description: TD Cowen analyst Lance Vitanza has revised the target price for Bitcoin reserve company MicroStrategy (MSTR) from $440 to $350, while maintaining a buy rating. According to Odaily, this adjustment reflects the analyst's updated assessment of the company's financial outlook.
